events.title: Festival International Nuits d'Afrique
events.description: World-music festival with free outdoor programming plus ticketed shows in downtown venues.

events.title: Just For Laughs / Juste pour rire
events.description: Comedy festival with English and French programming — a strong rainy-night option.
events.notes: Verify the exact English-language show before booking.

events.title: Chợ Đêm MTL — Vietnamese Night Market
events.description: Food market and cultural festival — street food, vendors, and performances, easy to enjoy without a fixed itinerary.

events.title: Festival Haïti en Folie
events.description: Haitian music, food, art, and cultural festival, particularly convenient when programming is at Parc La Fontaine.

events.title: Fierté Montréal
events.description: Major Pride festival with many free events; Community Days and the parade are easy entry points.

events.title: Otakuthon
events.description: Anime and gaming convention — Artist Alley, panels, and convention energy, fun even for casual fans.

events.title: MUTEK
events.description: Electronic music and digital-art festival — more immersive and unusual than a conventional concert.

events.title: Fantasia International Film Festival
events.description: Genre-film festival — horror, sci-fi, fantasy, and weird premieres, great for a playful movie night.

events.title: OSHEAGA
events.description: Montréal's biggest music festival — consider one day rather than committing to the full weekend.

events.title: POP Montréal
events.description: Indie music, film, art, talks, and markets in Plateau/Mile End-adjacent venues.
events.notes: Lineup still being announced — re-check closer to the dates.

events.title: UCI Road World Championships
events.description: International cycling championships with citywide atmosphere and free roadside spectating.
events.notes: Route and spectator details still need a refresh — re-check closer to September.

events.title: Piknic Électronik
events.description: Outdoor electronic music on summer Sundays — daytime and early evening, lower commitment than a multi-day festival.
events.notes: Check the weekly lineup.

events.title: Fireworks viewing — Jacques-Cartier Bridge / Old Port
events.description: Free fireworks viewing on competition nights — easy to combine with an Old Montréal dinner or waterfront walk.
events.notes: Verify show nights before going.

events.title: Cirque du Soleil — Montréal shows
events.description: Polished, memorable special night out when a normal event listing feels too ordinary.
events.notes: Exact production, dates, and prices need verification before booking.

events.title: PHI immersive-art programming
events.description: Immersive art and audio programming — a distinctive indoor date that's more experiential than a standard gallery visit.

events.title: Pointe-à-Callière — Alley-oop: An Interactive Adventure
events.description: A lighter, playful interactive museum exhibit that pairs well with a waterfront walk.

events.title: MMFA — Rising Suns
events.description: Indigenous art and history from the Great Lakes confederacies — one of the more substantial cultural outings currently on.

events.title: MMFA — Beyond the Pedestal
events.description: Long-running exhibition — a dependable rainy or cold-weekend fallback.

events.title: MMFA — Chiharu Shiota: The Soul Trembles
events.description: Major immersive installation-art exhibition — the strongest upcoming fall date on the list.
events.notes: Opens September 26.

events.title: McCord Stewart — Céline in Dior: A Dazzling Moment
events.description: Fashion and pop-culture exhibition with broad appeal — a lighter museum option.

events.title: McCord Stewart — Montréal 1976: An Olympic Feat
events.description: Local history at the intersection of sports, design, and architecture.

events.title: McCord Stewart — On the Menu Montreal: A Restaurant Story
events.description: Montréal restaurant and food history — easy to turn into a complete evening by following it with dinner.

events.title: MMFA — The Torlonia Collection
events.description: Major Roman-sculpture exhibition in its final days.
events.notes: Closes July 19 — go now if interested.

events.title: MMFA — Richard Avedon: Immortal
events.description: Photography exhibition — an easier lift than a full permanent-collection day.

events.title: MMFA — Keerat Kaur: Sweet Speech
events.description: Contemporary exhibition, useful as part of a combined MMFA visit.

events.title: MMFA — Ready, Set, Go! Sports Inspire Art
events.description: Sports-meets-art exhibition, timely in a year of major Montréal sports events.

events.title: Pointe-à-Callière — Heroes of Greece: The Age of Troy
events.description: History and archaeology of Greek heroes and Troy — easy to combine with dinner and a waterfront walk.

events.title: Pointe-à-Callière — Montréal 1976: The Olympic Legacy
events.description: Olympic-legacy exhibition that pairs directly with the other 1976 and sports-art shows.

events.title: Randolph Pub Ludique
events.description: Board-game pub with a large library and staff recommendations — an easy two-person date without planning ahead.
events.notes: Check current cover and food policy.

events.title: La Récréation
events.description: Board-game café/bar — games without organizing a group at home.

events.title: L'Abyss
events.description: Gaming pub and nerd-culture venue — more social and programming-oriented than a conventional board-game café.
events.notes: Check the event calendar.

events.title: Montreal board-game Meetup groups
events.description: Recurring board-game nights — one of the better routes to Anglo- and newcomer-friendly events.
events.notes: Quality and schedule vary — start with a one-off night.

events.title: Montreal Ultimate
events.description: Recreational and competitive ultimate frisbee — social and active without requiring an elite athletic background.
events.notes: Check the registration window.

events.title: Jarry Park beach volleyball courts
events.description: Public beach-volleyball courts — the best informal summer volleyball lead near the Plateau, easy by bike or Metro.
events.notes: Facility confirmed, but organized pickup is not — check Meetup/Facebook groups.

events.title: Sports Montréal
events.description: Adult sports, fitness, swimming, tennis, and aquatics — broad, approachable structured programming.
events.notes: Re-check offerings each session.

events.title: UQAM Centre sportif — volleyball lead
events.description: Transit-friendly indoor-sport facility, plausible for public free-play, courses, or leagues.
events.notes: Strong lead, not a confirmed public program — verify non-student access. Also see citadins.uqam.ca.

events.title: CEPSUM (Université de Montréal) — volleyball lead
events.description: Strong indoor-sport infrastructure with reasonable transit access — a candidate for structured play or court rental.
events.notes: Strong lead, not a confirmed current adult program.

events.title: Loisirs Montréal (city recreation portal)
events.description: The broadest source for city-run activities — often among the cheapest options.
events.notes: Search interface can be clunky; listings vary in language.

events.title: Club Sportif MAA
events.description: Private athletic and wellness club — the better fit when amenities matter more than price.
events.notes: Premium; check membership and trial pricing.

events.title: YMCAs of Québec
events.description: Fitness, aquatics, community programs, and adult classes — practical and unintimidating.
events.notes: Check branch-specific schedules.

events.title: Montreal Shamrocks GAC
events.description: Gaelic football, hurling, and Irish sports club — an English-friendly, distinctive route into an athletic community.
events.notes: Seasonal/club-based; check beginner welcome and training nights.

events.title: Bixi weeknight activity loop
events.description: Self-guided bike loop — no registration, low commitment, and very Montréal.
events.notes: Best May–October.

events.title: Parc Sir-Wilfrid-Laurier
events.description: One of the closest multi-purpose parks: pool, ping pong, badminton, and picnic space.
events.notes: Re-check pool access and seasonal schedules.

events.title: Parc Jeanne-Mance
events.description: Tennis, soccer, skating, and direct Mount Royal access — extremely easy from the Plateau.
events.notes: Re-check ongoing park work and seasonal access.

events.title: Parc La Fontaine
events.description: The default walk, picnic, pond, theatre, or skating spot — pairs naturally with dinner on Mont-Royal, Duluth, or Rachel.
events.notes: Re-check seasonal programming.

events.title: Complexe sportif Claude-Robillard
events.description: Major sports complex — pool, track, and fitness for structured training beyond a casual park outing.
events.notes: Verify public access schedule.

events.title: Mount Royal loop
events.description: The best no-car hike substitute — views, forest, stairs, with cafés before or after.

events.title: Gault Nature Reserve / Mont Saint-Hilaire
events.description: Summit hiking over a lake — real nature without a huge drive; the strongest conventional hike on the list.
events.notes: Pass/reservation may be required; check trail conditions.

events.title: Parc national du Mont-Saint-Bruno
events.description: Forest-and-lakes provincial park — an approachable hike-and-picnic day, a short drive away.
events.notes: SEPAQ access/parking applies.

events.title: Parc national d'Oka
events.description: Beach plus hiking — better as a full summer day than a pure hiking trip.
events.notes: Seasonal; check beach and parking conditions.

events.title: Parc national des Îles-de-Boucherville
events.description: Flat island trails with biking, paddling, and picnics — nature and activity without a difficult trail.

events.title: Brutopia Brewpub — trivia lead
events.description: Central, casual brewpub — a low-friction first pub-quiz option.
events.notes: Strong lead; verify the current trivia night before going.

events.title: McKibbin's Irish Pub — trivia lead
events.description: Very English-friendly Irish pub with a traditional pub-quiz atmosphere.
events.notes: Strong lead; verify the branch schedule.

events.title: Ye Olde Orchard — trivia lead
events.description: English/Irish pub — useful for a destination night outside downtown and the Plateau.
events.notes: Strong lead; verify exact branch and schedule.

events.title: Hurley's Irish Pub — trivia lead
events.description: Comfortable, central, and very English-friendly classic Irish pub.
events.notes: Strong lead; verify current events.

events.title: Burgundy Lion — event lead
events.description: Polished British-style pub — a more deliberate dinner-and-pub night, though not Plateau-close.
events.notes: Strong lead; verify current events.

events.title: A/Maze Montréal
events.description: The most convenient first escape-room option — cooperative puzzle dates that work with just two people.
events.notes: Check minimum players and room language.

events.title: Escaparium
events.description: High-production destination escape rooms — the fit when you want something elaborate and are willing to travel.
events.notes: Check theme and travel time.

events.title: Ezkapaz
events.description: Escape-game venue — a useful comparison against A/Maze by theme and difficulty.
events.notes: Check current room details and language.

events.title: Sauve Qui Peut / Immersia
events.description: Backup escape-room options that expand the pool of themes, group sizes, and locations.
events.notes: Also see immersia.ca. Check horror intensity and minimum players.

events.title: Ottawa Bluesfest
events.description: Large multi-genre music festival in Ottawa (~2h drive) — major English-language festival atmosphere, easy to pair with central Ottawa.

events.title: Capital Pride (Ottawa)
events.description: Central, festive Pride festival in Ottawa with many free events — a low-friction English-friendly overnight.
events.notes: Parade anchor was August 30, 1:00–4:00 p.m. at last check.

events.title: Ottawa International Animation Festival
events.description: Distinctive animation and film festival — pairs well with central Ottawa museums and restaurants for a fall weekend.
events.notes: Strong lead; exact 2026 dates need verification.

events.title: CityFolk (Ottawa)
events.description: Folk, roots, and indie festival — easy to build around a Glebe walk, dinner, or overnight.
events.notes: Strong recurring lead; verify 2026 dates and lineup.

events.title: Ottawa Chamberfest
events.description: Chamber-music festival — the quieter cultural alternative when a large outdoor festival doesn't appeal.
events.notes: Strong recurring lead; verify dates and programme.

events.title: Escher / optical-illusion exhibit (watch)
events.location: TBD — PHI, OASIS, Arsenal, MMFA, or McCord
events.description: No confirmed Montréal Escher exhibition yet — tracked because impossible-geometry and optical-illusion shows are an exceptionally strong fit.
events.notes: Watch only — verify before planning.
